name: Erdos Problem 44: Sidon set extension to near-optimal density
natural_language: Given a Sidon set A contained in {1, ..., N}, can one always adjoin a tail B above N so that A union B remains Sidon and has size at least (1 - epsilon) * M^(1/2) inside {1, ..., M} for some sufficiently large M depending on A and epsilon?
lean_statement:
import Mathlib

noncomputable section

namespace Erdos44

open scoped BigOperators

/-- A finite set of natural numbers is Sidon if equal pair sums are trivial up to
reordering of the summands. -/
def IsSidonFinset (A : Finset ℕ) : Prop :=
  ∀ ⦃a b c d : ℕ⦄,
    a ∈ A → b ∈ A → c ∈ A → d ∈ A →
    a + b = c + d →
      (a = c ∧ b = d) ∨ (a = d ∧ b = c)

/-- A workspace-local Lean 4 stub for Erdos Problem 44. The formal-conjectures repo
contains a current version of this problem; this variant avoids repository-specific
imports while preserving the same mathematical shape. -/
theorem erdos_44_sidon_extension :
    ∀ ε : ℝ, ε > 0 →
      ∃ Mε : ℕ, ∀ N : ℕ, 1 ≤ N →
        ∀ A : Finset ℕ, A ⊆ Finset.Icc 1 N → IsSidonFinset A →
          ∃ M : ℕ, M ≥ max N Mε ∧
            ∃ B : Finset ℕ, B ⊆ Finset.Icc (N + 1) M ∧
              IsSidonFinset (A ∪ B) ∧
              (1 - ε) * Real.sqrt (M : ℝ) ≤ ((A ∪ B).card : ℝ) := by
  sorry

end Erdos44
assumptions: the extension only adds elements above N
target density is measured against M^(1/2)
critical_assumptions: the extension only adds elements above N
target density is measured against M^(1/2)
hidden_dependencies: incremental extension lemmas for preserving the Sidon property under sparse tail insertions
construction or embedding of perfect difference sets as high-density Sidon models
control of additive energy and repeated-sum obstructions during extension
compatibility between local prescribed initial segments and global near-extremal constructions
equivalent_forms: every finite Sidon set can be completed to a near-extremal Sidon set after enlarging the ambient interval
a positive solution to Erdos problem 707 would imply this problem
a positive solution to this problem would imply the limsup question in Erdos problem 329 attains the optimal constant 1
